threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the tube' instead of 'the Overground' and look lost when the bus is late. Welcome to the real Hackney, mate.
Caught the 59 last night, driver stopped for a guy selling homemade kebabs at 2 AM. Only in Brixton, I suppose.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One morning, I stopped at my local bakery and saw the owner brewing coffee for a couple of Turkish grandmas discussing politics. That's when it hit me; this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a late-night Späti serving currywurst and craft beer, while debating the latest techno tracks with the cashier. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot locals by how they casually avoid eye contact with the guy yelling about the robot uprising on 16th street.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a full tuxedo playing violin on a packed Muni bus, completely unfazed by the chaos of the Civic Center delays. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think all the locals are at the plaça for the paella, but half the crowd is just waiting for the vermut to kick in while discussing Barça.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still gawking at the colorful murals, taking snapshots of everything while awkwardly trying to decipher the metro map.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the street when you see a bus coming, instead of just stepping aside and waiting for it to glide past.
Caught the night bus from Peckham last week, and a guy was juggling oranges. Three stopped to film it, but only one clapped when he finished. Odd vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a pigeon stealing a slice of döner from a drunk tourist’s lap at Hermannplatz. That’s when I felt home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with make-shift bars and art installations. It's like a secret city under the city.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they still call it 'Embarcadero' and never say 'The Bay'—only tourists mix those up.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a hedgehog on the tracks at Civic Center yesterday. Muni stopped for 20 minutes while the driver insisted on ‘not leaving a friend behind.’

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the plaça is just for photos, but locals gather there to swap stories, not selfies. It’s our living room, not a backdrop.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still insist on using Google Maps for the simplest routes instead of just asking a local.
